subject: Chemistry
title: Bioluminescence: Living Light
description: A quick win: understand Bioluminescence: Living Light.

hook.text: Fireflies glow because luciferin reacts with oxygen inside their bodies, releasing energy as visible light, not heat.

buildup.text: The enzyme luciferase catalyzes the oxidation of luciferin. The excited product releases a photon as it relaxes.

discovery.text: Over 75% of deep-sea creatures are bioluminescent. In the dark ocean, making your own light is the norm.

twist.text: Bioluminescence is incredibly efficient—nearly 100% of energy becomes light. A lightbulb wastes 95% as heat.

climax.text: Scientists put luciferase genes into plants. Glow-in-the-dark tobacco was the first bioluminescent plant, made in 1986.

punchline.text: A chemical reaction that emits light, not heat. Nature's most efficient lamp.

quiz.question: What makes bioluminescence so energy-efficient?
options: Nearly 100% of the chemical energy is converted to light with almost no heat
The organisms recycle the light back into chemical energy
Bioluminescent reactions only last for microseconds
